headline: The Sedition Act Was Supposed to Be Gone by Now
context: Pakatan Harapan promised to repeal the Sedition Act 1948 within 100 days of taking office in 2018 — it remains fully enforceable in 2026.

cards.big: Promised repeal: Day 100. Current status: fully operational.
cards.sub: The Sedition Act 1948 has survived every government that pledged to abolish it.

cards.big: At least 128 charges filed under the Act since 2018.
cards.sub: Successive AGs have used it against opposition figures, activists, and journalists alike.
No mens rea required. The accused need not intend sedition — only produce material deemed seditious.

cards.big: Every coalition in power has found the Act useful.
cards.sub: BN used it. PH kept it. PN invoked it. Unity government: silent.

cards.big: Why does every government break the same promise?
cards.sub: The pattern is the answer: the Act protects whoever holds power from whoever challenges it.

cards.big: A law that every government promises to repeal and none does tells you more about power than about sedition.
